questions.question: Which combination of factors mainly determines the type of natural vegetation in a region?
questions.explanation: Climate and soil conditions mainly determine natural vegetation.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a type of natural vegetation found in India?
questions.explanation: Temperate desert forest is not a vegetation type found in India.

questions.question: Which of the following forests is most dense and multi-layered?
questions.explanation: Evergreen forests are dense and have multilayer vegetation.

questions.question: Which pair of trees is commonly found in tropical evergreen forests?
questions.explanation: Ebony and mahogany are common evergreen forest trees.

questions.question: Which vegetation type is adapted to survive in regions with rainfall less than 70 cm?
questions.explanation: Thorn forests grow in areas with very low rainfall.

questions.question: Which forest type changes according to altitude in mountainous regions?
questions.explanation: Montane forests vary with altitude in mountains.

questions.question: Which feature helps mangrove trees survive in tidal waters?
questions.explanation: Mangrove trees have breathing roots that help them survive in waterlogged soil.

questions.question: The Sundarbans mangrove forests are located in which delta?
questions.explanation: The Sundarbans are located in the Ganga-Brahmaputra delta.

questions.question: Which animal species is exclusively protected in Gir National Park?
questions.explanation: The Asiatic lion survives only in Gir National Park.

questions.question: Which medicinal plant is used to treat snake bites and blood pressure?
questions.explanation: Sarpagandha is widely used in traditional medicine.

questions.question: Which group of animals includes gharials and crocodiles?
questions.explanation: Crocodiles and gharials are reptiles.

questions.question: Which bird migrates thousands of kilometers to reach India in winter?
questions.explanation: Siberian cranes migrate to India during winter.

questions.question: Which law was passed in India to protect wildlife species?
questions.explanation: The Wildlife Protection Act was implemented in 1972.

questions.question: Which conservation project was launched specifically for tiger protection?
questions.explanation: Project Tiger was launched in 1973 to protect tigers.

questions.question: Which of the following is the main aim of biosphere reserves?
questions.explanation: Biosphere reserves conserve ecosystems and biodiversity.

questions.question: Which of the following biosphere reserves is located in southern India?
questions.explanation: Nilgiri Biosphere Reserve lies in southern India.

questions.question: Which of the following activities leads directly to habitat destruction?
questions.explanation: Deforestation destroys natural habitats.

questions.question: Which of the following ecosystems supports both terrestrial and aquatic life?
questions.explanation: Mangrove forests support both land and marine organisms.

questions.question: Which factor is responsible for variation in vegetation across mountain slopes?
questions.explanation: Vegetation changes with altitude due to climate variation.

questions.question: Which species is considered endangered in India?
questions.explanation: The tiger is considered an endangered species in India.

questions.question: Which vegetation type is most useful for protecting coastal regions from storms?
questions.explanation: Mangroves protect coasts from storms and erosion.

questions.question: Which of the following helps maintain ecological balance?
questions.explanation: Biodiversity maintains ecological balance.

questions.question: Which conservation method protects animals in their natural habitats?
questions.explanation: Wildlife sanctuaries protect animals in natural habitats.

questions.question: Which human activity has the greatest negative impact on forests?
questions.explanation: Deforestation destroys forests and ecosystems.

questions.question: Why is biodiversity conservation necessary?
questions.explanation: Conservation protects species and maintains ecological balance.
